_doc: Frozen calibration set for the gate stack. Authentic items are referenced by qid ONLY and loaded from data/parsed/*.json at runtime by load_evalset.py — never copied into the repo (they are UHR-copyrighted; see project CLAUDE.md legal note). Seeded-defect items are authored in seeded/*.json. Do not edit this file without bumping `version` and re-running the eval per run-protocol.md.
_changelog: 1.2.0 (2026-07-24): added seeded item elf-b0-016 — a structurally VALID 5-gap ELF cloze whose PASSAGE register is clickbait/blog listicle (reader address, hype exclamation, SEO subheads, social CTA). Intended_kill_gate G-REGISTER, no secondary: keys are solvable and the English is grammatical, so the kill isolates passage register. This seed also exercises the cloze-format scope rule (G-REGISTER.md, owner decision 2026-07-24) in the kill direction — proving the format exemption does not rubber-stamp non-HP cloze content. || 1.1.0 (2026-07-20): re-authored the DEFECT in both double-key seeds (elf-b0-004, las-b0-014) — passages/topics unchanged, questions/options rewritten. The 2026-07-20 eval run showed the planted second keys were attractive-but-beatable: three independent judges (both G-KEY blind runs + G-DISTRACTOR) each resolved a single clearly-correct answer, so the seeds could not test G-DISTRACTOR's sensitivity. Both now use a 'which statement is supported' stem where the key and one distractor restate different passage sentences with equal directness (no ranking delimiter); G-KEY registered as documented secondary since an honest blind solver reports MULTIPLE_DEFENSIBLE. || 1.0.2 (2026-07-20): lengthened las-b0-011's passage (130 -> ~225 words) by inserting a topic-consistent middle paragraph; the verbatim-lift sentence, questions, key, and defect are untouched. Cause: 011 was missed in the 1.0.1 lengthening pass, so once bands.json went calibrated=true it double-died on M-BANDS + M-PLAGIARISM (eval run 2026-07-20), muddying the intended single-gate attribution. || 1.0.1 (2026-07-20): lengthened 9 LÄS seeded passages (las-b0-001,003,005,006,007,008,013,014,015) by appending a topic-consistent closing paragraph. Defect locations, keys, and question text untouched. Cause: bands.json calibration (calibrate_bands.py) measured the real LÄS 'short' passage floor at ~188 words (widened); the original seed passages (118-175 words) were authored under the old placeholder bands (min 300) and were never validated against a real floor either, so once bands.json went calibrated=true they started mechanically dying on M-BANDS before reaching their intended LLM gate. See run-protocol.md's rerun-required rule 3 (mech.py/bands.json changed).

authentic_pass._intent: Must ALL receive final status SURVIVED (clean or flagged) — never DEAD. These are real HP items; any lethal-gate kill against one is a false positive and an eval failure.

seeded_defects._intent: Each must be killed by its intended_kill_gate (or, for the hard negative, must PASS). Loaded from seeded/<candidate_id>.json. The _seed block is stripped by the orchestrator before any gate dispatch.

items._note: genuine double key; an honest blind solver may report MULTIPLE_DEFENSIBLE, so a G-KEY kill is a legitimate secondary.

REGISTER-BROKEN CLOZE: structurally valid 5-gap ELF cloze, keys solvable and English grammatical, but the passage is clickbait/blog listicle. No secondary — the kill must isolate passage register (not G-KEY/G-ENG). Asserts the cloze-format scope rule (G-REGISTER.md 2026-07-24) does not exempt register.
HARD NEGATIVE: attractive distractors, no real defect; must PASS. Catches over-firing G-DISTRACTOR / mis-pulled G-KEY.
genuine double key; an honest blind solver may report MULTIPLE_DEFENSIBLE, so a G-KEY kill is a legitimate secondary.

exemplar_pool._intent: Separate authentic qids that G-REGISTER agents load as comparison exemplars. Kept DISJOINT from authentic_pass so the register gate never compares an item to itself. Grouped by section and rough length class.
